name: Bay Networks' Switch Node Routing Switch Tackles Routing Performance Bottlenecks Head-On
description: Aberdeen analyst Virginia Brooks examines Bay Networks' Switch Node Routing Switch, announced March 3, 1997, as a Layer 3 switching solution for congested campus networks. The study analyzes how enterprise network traffic patterns had reversed from the traditional 80/20 local/cross-subnet model to 20/80, making router bottlenecks critical. Aberdeen assesses the Switch Node's architecture—separating data plane and control plane on dual 1.2 Gbps buses with IP AutoLearn—as a cost-effective alternative to router upgrades or full ATM migration.
